name: settings-file-day-basis-ui
description: Add file_day_basis control to Settings auto-buy section. Allows selecting which day's file to use for purchase (e.g. Sunday file for Monday purchase).
instruction:
In my_customers/frontend/src/app/itrp/settings/page.tsx AutoSchedulesSection:
1. After the DayPillSelector for auto-buy, add a Select dropdown for file_day_basis
2. Options: None (use current day), Monday, Tuesday, ..., Sunday
3. Track dirty state like other fields
4. Include in PUT /api/schedule/{country} payload (already in backend schema)
5. Show current value from schedule?.file_day_basis
acceptance_criteria: file_day_basis dropdown visible in auto-buy schedule section
Changing value marks form as dirty
Save sends file_day_basis to backend PUT endpoint

name: settings-auto-load-placeholder-with-info
description: Replace disabled auto-load section with clear info message explaining it's not available until data pipeline is implemented.
instruction:
In settings/page.tsx AutoSchedulesSection:
1. Replace the disabled toggle+pills for auto-load with an Alert component (Ant Design)
2. Message: 'Auto-load scheduling will be available once the Warsaw data pipeline integration is complete. File loading is currently manual via the Maintenance page.'
3. Type: info
4. This is better than a disabled toggle which looks like a bug
acceptance_criteria: Auto-load section shows info Alert instead of disabled controls
Message explains why it's not available

name: settings-country-rbac-filtering
description: Filter country dropdown in Settings page based on user permissions. OpCo-scoped user should only see their country.
instruction:
In settings/page.tsx:
1. Use useAuthStore to get allowedCountries()
2. If allowedCountries() returns a set, filter COUNTRY_OPTIONS to only those
3. If returns null (global access), show all countries
4. Same pattern as customers page
acceptance_criteria: OpCo user with scope_country=PL only sees PL in country dropdown

name: decision-multi-currency-schedule
description: Document decision: single schedule per country (current) vs per-currency schedule (documentation). Current code is simpler, doc spec may be over-engineered for 11 countries where 1 country = 1 currency.
instruction: Record decision in Forge: keep single schedule per country. Reasoning: 1. Each ITRP country has one primary currency, 2. Current opco_schedules stores one record per country which is sufficient, 3. Multi-currency UI adds complexity without clear business value, 4. If needed later, can extend opco_schedules with currency field.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded in Forge with reasoning

name: verify-o003
description: Deep-verify O-003: Settings page has all functional controls, file_day_basis works, auto-load has clear placeholder, RBAC filtering works.
instruction:
1. ruff check frontend files
2. Verify file_day_basis dropdown renders
3. Verify auto-load shows info message
4. Verify country dropdown respects user scope
5. Update O-003 KR progress
acceptance_criteria: All Settings controls functional or clearly marked as placeholder
